description: A spider leaves a 'niro' between two branches: a structure of interconnected strands. The word also covers a made web-like form, and tension or movement in one strand may pass through its connections. 'mera' (net) is a flexible open mesh used to hold, carry, or separate, while 'phaliso' (network) concerns the relations among connected points or participants.
articulatory_notes: Touch the tongue tip to the backs of the upper teeth for dental 'n', carry the voice through the nose, and move into stressed high-front unrounded 'i'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue tip at the alveolar ridge for 'r', then round the lips and finish with true-mid back 'o'.
usage_notes: The ordinary sense includes a spider's web and other physical strand structures. A metaphorical use works when those strands and transmitted relations matter to the image; 'phaliso' is the plainer word for a social, technical, or ecological network. Exact named digital services and protocols retain their source identities.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: A web makes tension, breakage, repair, and connection visible at once. Watching one closely teaches through the actual strands rather than through a borrowed theory of interdependence.

semantic_domains.nature: an interconnected strand structure made by a spider or found in a living setting
semantic_domains.physical: a web-like structure whose joined strands carry tension or movement
semantic_domains.spatial: strands arranged through a connected open structure
